commit.message:
Rewrite analysis tab with 90-day on-chain backfill (separate from paper trading)

- New backfill system: fetches all tx signatures from Alchemy RPC, parses
  pre/post token balances to identify actual swaps, caches to JSON on disk

- 5 scenario cards: Their Actual P&L, Our Copy (Ideal), +1/+2/+4 slots
- Cumulative P&L chart with all 5 lines (their actual + 4 copy scenarios)
- Round trip table and per-trade log with delay cost breakdown
- Time filters: 7d/30d/60d/90d
- Bridge serves backfill data via /api/analysis/{wallet}

commit.message:
perf: skip re-uploading unchanged images/videos on deploy

Use --no-overwrite for public/images/ and public/videos/ in lftp mirror.
Previously --only-newer compared CI-generated timestamps (always fresh),
causing all images to re-upload on every push. Now images/videos are only
uploaded if they don't already exist on the server.

commit.message:
Initial commit: Password Store Web - E2E encrypted password manager

- Next.js 16 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui
- E2E encryption using Web Crypto API (AES-256-GCM, PBKDF2)
- Auth flow with client-side key derivation (server never sees plaintext)
- JWT auth with httpOnly cookies, brute force protection
- Prisma ORM with PostgreSQL (Neon)
- Pages: register, login, dashboard, vault detail
- API routes: auth, vaults CRUD, credentials CRUD, password generator
- Recovery key system for account recovery
- Deployed to Vercel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[REFACTOR #243]: Java Cognitive Complexity 감소 (java:S3776) (#334)

* [REFACTOR #243]: Java Cognitive Complexity 감소 — 6개 서비스 메서드 CC ≤ 15 리팩토링

중첩 로직을 private 메서드로 추출하여 Cognitive Complexity 감소:
- MessageParsingService: traverseHeaderChain() 추출
- MessageTestService: buildErrorResponse(), sendSimulationRequest(), resolveInstance() 추출
- TransDataFileService: processSingleUpload(), executeSectionStatements() 추출

- WasPropertyService: copySingleProperty(), processBatchItem() 추출

Public API 시그니처 변경 없음. 111 tests pass, BUILD SUCCESS.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: exclude script itself from process killing

The script was killing itself because pgrep -f "modbridge" was
matching the modbridge.sh script file. Now using pgrep -x "modbridge"
to only match the exact process name, not the script. Also added
pidof as a fallback method.

This fixes the issue where the installation script would terminate
itself during cleanup.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: state store, storage, and data management scalability improvements (#655)

H2: Redis Save now uses append-only delta RPUSH for messages instead of
DEL+RPUSH, avoiding full list rewrites on every save. Falls back to full
rewrite only when message count decreases (truncation).

H3: Redis conversation listing now uses a global SET index instead of
SCAN, reducing list-all from O(keyspace) to O(conversations). The index
is maintained on Save/Delete with SCAN fallback for legacy data.

M1: FileEventStore file handles now use LRU eviction (default 256) to
prevent unbounded growth of open file descriptors.

M3: InMemoryIndex (vector search) now has a configurable max conversation
count with LRU eviction (default 1000) to bound memory.

M14: Annotation FileStore.Get() now maintains an in-memory ID-to-session
index for O(1) lookups instead of scanning all session files.

M15: Annotation FileStore file handles now use LRU eviction (default 256).

M16: FileStore dedup index race fixed by holding write lock from check
through write, preventing concurrent duplicate writes.

L1: Event store query now pre-builds a map from filter types for O(1)
type matching instead of linear scan per event.

L9: Added TODO comments on unbounded dedup/refcount maps in FileStore
noting they are acceptable for current usage.

L10: FileBlobStore known-hash map now uses LRU eviction (default 10000)
to bound memory growth.

All fixes include new unit tests. Internal LRU cache package added at
runtime/internal/lru/ for shared use across packages.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix daemon: non-blocking spawn, skip in test binaries

ensureDaemon was blocking for 10s polling for the daemon socket, causing
CI integration tests to timeout. Redesigned: connectDaemon only tries an
existing socket; spawnDaemon fires-and-forgets a background daemon for
future calls. NewClient falls back to in-process immediately when no
daemon is running. Also skips spawning from test binaries (*.test).

low speed, preventing the rider from immediately snapping back onto the
same rail in a loop.

commit.message:
fix(music): break cascading update storm across playback component tree

Five targeted fixes to eliminate the "Maximum update depth exceeded" crash:

1. UnifiedHeader: Replace useSearch() with individual Zustand selectors.
   Prevents re-renders when registerSearch() creates a new config object
   on every tab focus — was cascading through the entire render tree.

2. useTrackPlayback.handlePlayTrack: Move currentTrack and isPlaying to
   refs, reducing useCallback deps from 19 to 16. The callback is now
   stable across PlaybackContext updates, preventing the auto-advance
   setInterval from being constantly torn down and recreated.

3. useCastPlayback: Move currentTrack to ref in both effects. Three
   instances of this hook (DiscoverScreen, MiniPlayer, QueueAutoAdvance)
   were each re-running effects on every track change unnecessarily.

4. PlaybackContext.isCurrentTrack: Stabilize with ref-based currentTrack
   read. Previously depended on [currentTrack], causing the stateValue
   memo to change even when only the callback identity changed.

5. AudioPlayerContext: Add event-based usePlayerPlayingChange() hook.
   Replaces the unreliable player.playing-in-effect-deps approach for
   phase sync. Player state changes through our memoized context don't
   trigger consumer re-renders, making deps-based detection fragile.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(pds): distinguish pre-existing findings from new regressions

evaluate_gate() now checks whether findings are directly in changed
files before classifying them as blocking. Findings only reachable
via blast radius (pre-existing in untouched code) are downgraded to
warnings with a 'downgraded_from' annotation for traceability.

This prevents false-positive blocks where pre-existing gap_detection
findings in the blast radius would block pushes of unrelated changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix skinned normal transform and lighting in Lesson 32 (#174)

The vertex shader transformed normals by skin_mat only, producing
mesh-local normals instead of world-space normals.  Because the full
glTF joint matrix formula includes inv(meshWorld), the skin matrix
outputs mesh-local coordinates — the model matrix (which carries the
walk-path rotation) must be applied afterward.  Without it, dot(N, L)
computed the wrong angle and the lighting appeared to rotate with the
character.

Changes:
- skin.vert.hlsl: apply model matrix to skinned normal and world_pos
- skin.frag.hlsl: reduce shader shadow bias (hardware bias handles it)
- main.c: rotate light 180° around Y, add hardware depth bias to
  shadow pipeline, fix camera comment
- README.md: add "Skinned normals and the model matrix" section with
  diagram, broken-vs-correct code, and explanation
- gpu_diagrams.py: add diagram_skinned_normal_transform() two-panel
  visualization (broken mesh-local vs correct world-space normals)
- __main__.py: register new diagram under gpu/32
- Recompile SPIRV/DXIL for skin.vert and skin.frag
- Update screenshot at frame 20 for better 3/4 angle
